'''Abagi Bienose Ekoku''' (born 13 April 1966) is the interim [[CEO]] of the [[RFL]], a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer and a former [[track and field]] athlete in both [[discus]] and [[shot put]].<ref>[https://www.rugby-league.com/article/64695/abi-ekoku-to-leave-role-as-interim-rfl-ceo Abi Ekoku to leave role as interim RFL CEO]</ref> 

He is due to leave his role at the [[Rugby Football League]] at the end of April 2026 in order to take on the role as Chief Executive of [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Maguire |first=Tom |date=2026-04-09 |title=St.Helens R.F.C. appoint Abi Ekoku as CEO |url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/2026/04/09/st-helens-r-f-c-appoint-abi-ekoku-as-ceo/ |access-date=2026-04-09 |website=St.Helens R.F.C. |language=en-GB}}</ref>

== Sporting career ==
=== Athletics ===
Ekoku represented Great Britain at the [[European Athletics Championships]] and was National Champion in the [[Discus]] in 1990 and 1992<ref name="GBR athletics UK results"/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nuts.org.uk/Champs/AAA/index.htm |title=AAA, WAAA and National Championships Medallists |website=National Union of Track Statisticians |access-date=27 June 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.gbrathletics.com/bc/aaa.htm |title=AAA Championships (men) |website=GBR Athletics |access-date=27 June 2025}}</ref>. He represented [[England at the 1990 Commonwealth Games|England]] in Discus and Shot Put at the [[1990 Commonwealth Games]] in [[Auckland]], New Zealand.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://teamengland.org/commonwealth-games-history/auckland-1990/athletes|title=1990 Athletes|website=Team England}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://thecgf.com/results/games/3042/19/all|title=England team in 1990|website=Commonwealth Games Federation|access-date=8 October 2019|archive-date=4 April 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190404120931/https://thecgf.com/results/games/3042/19/all|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://thecgf.com/results/athletes/38471|title=Athletes and results|website=Commonwealth Games Federation}}</ref><ref>[http://www.sporting-heroes.net/athletics-heroes/stats_athletics/commonwealth/1990_m.asp 1990 Commonwealth Results at sporting heroes]</ref>

=== Rugby league ===
Ekoku switched to professional [[rugby league]] football for the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993-4 season]] and played for six seasons on the {{rlp|wg}}. He started his rugby league career with the [[London Broncos#1991–1994: London Crusaders|London Crusaders]], playing on a part-time basis whilst also lecturing in further education. He was signed by [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in July 1995, and played in the inaugural [[Super League]] season with the club. He joined the [[Bradford Bulls]] in 1997, helping the club win the Super League that year, and also played in the [[1997 Challenge Cup]] against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley]].

Ekoku announced his retirement from playing in 1998 and enjoyed a successful spell as chairman of the Rugby League Players' Association. He left the post in 1999 and was Managing Director at the [[Keighley Cougars]] before being appointed by the [[Bradford Bulls]] as Chief Executive Officer. Ekoku oversaw Bradford from 2000 to 2003 during a golden period in the Club’s history, when they won two [[Super League]] titles, two [[Challenge Cup]] finals and the [[World Club Challenge|World Club Championship]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Maguire |first=Tom |date=2026-04-09 |title=St.Helens R.F.C. appoint Abi Ekoku as CEO |url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/2026/04/09/st-helens-r-f-c-appoint-abi-ekoku-as-ceo/ |access-date=2026-04-09 |website=St.Helens R.F.C. |language=en-GB}}</ref>

Ekoku returned to the game in 2005 as an anti-doping tutor before being appointed team manager for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in 2006. He stepped down from this role at the end of 2007 after 10 international fixtures, including the [[2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2006 Gillette Tri-Nations Series]] and the successful [[Baskerville Shield|home series against New Zealand in 2007]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Ekoku ends tenure as GB manager|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7104023.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=28 January 2015|date=20 November 2007}}</ref>

After a period as a Business Development & Strategy Consultant in property development and leisure investment, Ekoku once again returned to the sport in March 2025, joining the club-led Strategic Review Panel at the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=RFL Board and Strategic Review Committee |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/63891/rfl-board-and-strategic-review-committee |access-date=2026-04-09 |website=www.rugby-league.com |language=en}}</ref> In October 2025 he was appointed as interim CEO of the RFL, during which time he oversaw the resolution of the administrations of [[Salford RLFC|Salford Red Devils]] and [[Halifax Panthers]], guiding both clubs to new ownership and a return to participation in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=2025-12-22 |title=Salford Red Devils' new owners confirmed as winning bid revealed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-new-owners-confirmed-as-winning-bid-revealed-by-rfl |access-date=2026-04-09 |website=Love Rugby League |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Three consortia in line for Halifax Panthers revival bid – Total Rugby League |url=https://www.totalrl.com/three-consortiums-in-line-for-halifax-panthers-takeover-bid/ |access-date=2026-04-09 |language=en-US}}</ref> In April 2026, Ekoku was appointed as CEO of [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens R.F.C]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2026-04-09 |title=Abi Ekoku: St Helens appoint interim RFL CEO as chief executive |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c20d8xddzy9o |access-date=2026-04-09 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> 

==Personal life==
Ekoku's brother [[Efan Ekoku|Efan]] is a former professional [[Association football|footballer]] who represented the [[Nigeria national football team]], and as of 2011 is a football pundit with [[ESPN]].
